name: MH-1: Trial filter predicate
description: Add _is_valid_trial(data) to signals.py that requires agent_info key in result.json and rejects harness summaries. Exclude directories matching __archived_invalid, __incomplete, __pre_sgenv_fix, __verifier_path_bug, __doubled_prefix patterns. Wire into extract_all() so it skips invalid trials. Add has_verifier_result bool field to TrialSignals.
acceptance: Function _is_valid_trial(data: dict) -> bool exists in signals.py
Returns False when data has no 'agent_info' key
Returns True when data has 'agent_info' key and dir is not excluded
extract_all() calls _is_valid_trial() and skips invalid trials
Directories matching __archived_invalid, __incomplete, __pre_sgenv_fix, __verifier_path_bug, __doubled_prefix are excluded
Tests cover: harness summary rejection, valid trial acceptance, excluded dir patterns, extract_all filtering
python3 -m pytest tests/test_signals.py passes with 0 failures

name: MH-2: Nullable reward + provenance flags
description: Change reward in TrialSignals from float default-to-0.0 to float|None when no verifier result exists. Add has_verifier_result: bool field to TrialSignals. Update extract_signals() to set reward=None and has_verifier_result=False when no verifier_result in result.json. Update annotate_trial() and all 32 heuristic checkers to handle None reward gracefully (skip reward-dependent heuristics when reward is None). Update classifier signals_to_features() to handle None reward. Update ensemble and report to handle None reward.
acceptance: TrialSignals TypedDict includes has_verifier_result: bool field
extract_signals() sets reward=None when no verifier_result in result.json
extract_signals() sets has_verifier_result=False when no verifier_result
annotate_trial() skips reward-dependent heuristics when reward is None instead of crashing
classifier.signals_to_features() handles None reward (converts to 0.0 for feature vector)
No TypeError or comparison errors when reward is None throughout the pipeline
All existing tests updated to pass: python3 -m pytest passes with 0 failures

name: MH-5: OpenHands tool registry
description: Create OPENHANDS_REGISTRY in tool_registry.py mapping str_replace_editor -> edit_tools, execute_bash -> search_tools (shell), browser -> code_nav_tools. Add a get_registry_for_agent(agent_name: str) function that returns OPENHANDS_REGISTRY for openhands agents and DEFAULT_REGISTRY otherwise. Wire auto-selection into extract_signals() based on agent_info.name in result.json.
acceptance: OPENHANDS_REGISTRY exists in tool_registry.py with str_replace_editor in edit_tools and execute_bash in search_tools
get_registry_for_agent('openhands') returns OPENHANDS_REGISTRY
get_registry_for_agent('claude-code') returns DEFAULT_REGISTRY
extract_signals() auto-selects registry from agent_info.name when tool_registry is not explicitly passed
Tests cover registry creation, agent-based selection, and signal extraction with OpenHands tools
python3 -m pytest tests/test_tool_registry.py tests/test_signals.py passes with 0 failures

name: MH-4: MANIFEST.json integration
description: Add a load_manifest(path) function that parses MANIFEST.json to build a suite_mapping dict. Add directory-name convention fallback for benchmark resolution (e.g. csb_crossrepo_* -> crossrepo, openhands_* -> openhands). Add benchmark_source field to TrialSignals indicating 'manifest', 'directory', or empty. Wire into extract_signals() via the existing benchmark_resolver parameter.
acceptance: load_manifest(path) function exists and returns a dict usable as suite_mapping
Directory-name convention fallback resolves common prefixes: csb_crossrepo -> crossrepo, openhands -> openhands
benchmark_source field added to TrialSignals: 'manifest', 'directory', or empty string
Tests cover: manifest parsing, directory-name fallback, benchmark_source provenance
python3 -m pytest tests/test_signals.py passes with 0 failures

name: MH-3: Trajectory-aware denominators in reporting
description: Add requires_trajectory metadata to heuristic checkers (derived from taxonomy signal_dependencies). Update generate_report() to show separate sections: 'Trajectory-Dependent Categories' with denominator=trajectory-available trials, and 'Reward-Dependent Categories' with denominator=full corpus. Ensure retrieval_failure rate is computed against trajectory-available trials only.
acceptance: Each heuristic checker has a requires_trajectory classification (via metadata dict or taxonomy lookup)
generate_report() output markdown shows 'Trajectory-Dependent Categories' section with correct denominator
generate_report() output markdown shows 'Reward-Dependent Categories' section with correct denominator
retrieval_failure rate uses trajectory-available trials as denominator, not full corpus
Report JSON includes denominator info: {category: {count: N, denominator: M, rate: N/M}}
Tests cover: denominator splitting, retrieval_failure denominator, JSON structure
python3 -m pytest tests/test_report.py tests/test_annotator.py passes with 0 failures

name: SH-1: JSONL output format
description: Update extract_all() and annotate CLI commands to support .jsonl output (one JSON object per line). Write a sidecar .meta.json with schema_version, taxonomy_version, generated_at. CLI commands detect format from output file extension (.json for legacy, .jsonl for new). Add read helpers that load both formats transparently.
acceptance: extract CLI writes .jsonl when output path ends in .jsonl
Each line of .jsonl is a valid JSON object (one trial per line)
A .meta.json sidecar is written alongside .jsonl with schema_version, taxonomy_version, generated_at
CLI extract command works with both .json and .jsonl output paths
CLI annotate command works with both .json and .jsonl input/output
Helper function load_signals(path) transparently reads both formats
Tests cover: jsonl write/read roundtrip, meta sidecar content, format detection
python3 -m pytest tests/test_cli.py tests/test_signals.py passes with 0 failures

name: SH-2: observatory ingest command
description: Add 'observatory ingest' CLI subcommand that runs filter -> extract -> enrich (from MANIFEST) -> write JSONL in one command. Accepts --manifest, --runs-dir, --output, --state flags. Incremental mode: tracks {trial_path, mtime, size} in a state JSON file, only re-extracts changed trials on subsequent runs.
acceptance: 'observatory ingest --runs-dir X --output Y' command exists and works
ingest writes JSONL output with one trial per line
ingest uses _is_valid_trial to filter harness summaries
ingest accepts --manifest flag and uses it for benchmark resolution
ingest accepts --state flag for incremental mode
Running ingest twice with no changes produces identical output (mtime skip)
Tests cover: basic ingest, incremental skip, manifest flag, state file creation
python3 -m pytest tests/test_cli.py passes with 0 failures

name: SH-3+SH-4: Co-occurrence matrix and dimension aggregation
description: Add category co-occurrence matrix computation (pairwise counts + phi coefficients) to report.py. Add dimension-level aggregation that rolls up categories to parent dimensions using taxonomy_v3.yaml. Output co-occurrence as symmetric matrix (JSON). Add 'Dimension Summary' section to generate_report() showing failure rate per dimension.
acceptance: co_occurrence_matrix(annotations) function returns symmetric dict-of-dicts with phi coefficients
Matrix diagonal contains category prevalence counts
Off-diagonal phi coefficients are in [-1, 1]
dimension_aggregation(annotations, taxonomy) returns per-dimension failure rates
generate_report() includes 'Dimension Summary' section in markdown
Report JSON includes dimension_summary key with {dimension: {failure_rate, trial_count}} structure
Tests cover: matrix shape, phi coefficient bounds, dimension rollup, report section presence
python3 -m pytest tests/test_report.py passes with 0 failures
